tools.name: trading_data
tools.description: Market Sensor. Supports Live (Massive API) and Backtest (StockBench Parquet) modes. Calculates RSI, EMA, SMA, and Alpha.

args.--period: Integer value for the unit. Default: 1.
args.--rsi: Integer period for RSI calculation (standard is 14).
args.--ema: Integer period for EMA calculation (standard is 20).
args.--sma: Integer period for SMA calculation (standard is 100).
args.--backtest: Flag: If set, loads historical data from local Parquet files.
args.--date: YYYY-MM-DD: The simulated 'current' date (Required if --backtest is used).

tools.output_format: JSON: {ticker, date, technical_analysis: {price, rsi, ema, sma, alpha}, fundamental_data: {}}

tools.name: memory_gate
tools.description: Agentic Memory System (TITAN/MIRAS). Manages Surprise-Gating, Decay, and Hierarchical Retrieval.

args.--query: Flag: If set, returns the 3-Level MIRAS Scene Summary instead of logging.
args.--ticker: Ticker symbol (Required for both query and logging).
args.--price: Current price of the ticker (Required for logging).
args.--ma: Moving Average (EMA/SMA) of the ticker (Required for logging).
args.--spy_price: Current price of SPY (Required for Level 1 Macro query).
args.--spy_ma: Moving Average of SPY (Required for Level 1 Macro query).
args.--action: The action taken: 'BUY', 'SELL', or 'SKIP'. Default: SKIP.
args.--reason: The textual logic/monologue explaining the action.

tools.output_format: Structured MIRAS Hierarchy Scene Summary string.

tools.name: sanity_checker
tools.description: Governance Layer. Validates trade proposals against budget and hallucination safety rails.
tools.constraints: Strict compliance required. You MUST NOT execute if status is 'REJECTED'.

tools.name: trade_executor
tools.description: Execution Desk. Updates portfolio (account.json) and history (trade_history.db).

args.--quantity: Integer: Number of shares to trade.

tools.output_format: Success message and current account balance.

tools.name: performance_updater
tools.description: Updates competitive benchmarks. Use --backtest and --date when running historical simulations to ensure the auditor uses synchronized Parquet data.

args.--backtest: Boolean flag. If present, reads from local StockBench Parquet files.
args.--date: The target end-date for the 30-day performance window (YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: auditor
tools.description: Strategic Evolution & Quantitative Auditor (Pillar 4). Benchmarks the agent's internal trade_history.db and account.json against 'The Masters' (Institutional Elite) and 'AI Sprinters' to identify logic gaps and strategic decay.

args.--lookback: Optional: Number of days to include in the audit (e.g., '7d', '30d'). Default: All history.
args.--mode: Optional: 'summary' for raw metrics or 'evolution' for the full LLM strategic critique. Default: evolution.
args.Internal_Input_Notice: This tool automatically ingests local trade_history.db and competitors.json. No manual data entry required.

tools.output_format: JSON Audit Report: Contains Total Return, Market Alpha, Peer Ranking, and a 'Strategic Expert Critique' for logic re-weighting.
